meaning: vt.使(某人,其神经,其情绪)平静,安慰,使(痛苦,疼痛)缓和或减轻
examples: It uses therapy animals to soothe nervous passengers.
He became as wild as a beast and no one could soothe him.
Many people soothe their consciences by purchasing only free-range fowl and eggs, blissfully ignorant that "free range" has very little practical significance.
Many protesters marched carrying supplies of lemons and milk, to soothe the effects of tear gas.
To soothe the uninvited, a second and third were presented as ad-hoc responses to market turmoil.
